title: Making electro-competent E. coli cells and transformation of them
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9kbc7","text":"This protocol describes a method to makeEscherichia colicells electro-competent. The method involves one overnight culturing step, followed by several hours of culturing during the day and 1.5 - 2 hours of preparation of electro-competent cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":40,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"692cb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7d8bg","text":"The protocol also describes how to do the transformation (electroporation) afterwards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"52ts5","text":"-  The reason for resuspending the cells in 5 ml glycerol solution first before adding the remainder of the 50 ml is that it is easier to resuspend in a smaller volume than in a completely filled tube.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9r0r3","text":"-  Competent cells can be stored for 6 to 12 months at -80°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4o2p","text":"-  Work sterile during the whole procedure (next to bunsen burner or in flow cabinet)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9usnd","text":"-  After transformation, the cells are spread onto a selective plate. To assure that single colonies appear, two plates can be used instead: one to spread 1/10 of the cells onto, and another one to spread 9/10 of the cells onto. If the transformation was very efficient, the single colonies will appear on the 1/10 plate and a lawn will appear on the 9/10 plate. In the case of a less efficient transformation, single colonies will appear on both plates or only on the 9/10 plate (and nothing on the 1/10 plate).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bmlb5","text":"-  Be careful when using needles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"12c9n","text":"- Avoid skin contact with N2(l), it can cause cold burns.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":27,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ek6al","text":"-  All materials that came into contact with transformed E. colishould be autoclaved before being disposed of. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":57,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: 400 mL, LB/ w/o salt in 1 Liter erlenmeyer flask + overnight culture 0.2 Genome copies per ml, OD600 Original: Next day, first thing in the morning, inoculate 400 ml LB w/o salt in a 1 l Erlenmeyer flask with enough of the overnight culture to reach OD600 of 0.2 and incubate for ~3 hours to OD600 0.5-1.0.
0 °C, ice 00:15:00 Original: Transfer culture volume to 8 Greiner tubes of 50 ml and cool on ice for 15 minutes.
Centrifuge for 00:10:00, 2000 g 4 °C
Discard supernatant and add 5 mL of 10 % volume, glycerol , resuspend the pellet in it and then add 45 mL of 10 % volume, glycerol , shake by hand a few times to mix.
Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 2000g and 4°C.
Discard supernatant and add 5ml of 10% glycerol, resuspend the pellet in it and then add 45ml of 10% glycerol, shake by hand a few times to mix.
Put on ice for 10 minutes.
Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 2000g and 4°C.
Discard supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 ml ice-cold 10% glycerol, pool the contents of all 8 tubes into one tube.
Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 1500g and 4°C.
Discard supernatant and add 400 μl 10% glycerol (this will result in ~800 μl suspension).
Make aliquots of 40μl in pre-cooled eppendorf tubes and flash freeze with N2(l). Store at -80°C.
Inoculate 5 mL, LB w/o salt in 50ml tube + 2 µL, E.coli + 37 °C + 12:00:00, Overnight Original: Inoculate 5 ml LB w/o saltin a 50 ml culturing tube withE. coliand incubate overnight at 37°C / 250 rpm
1.Next day, first thing in the morning, inoculate 400 ml LB w/o salt in a 1 l Erlenmeyer flask with enough of the overnight culture to reach OD600 of 0.2 and incubate for ~3 hours to OD600 0.5-1.0.
2.Pre-cool centrifuge to 4°C.
3.Transfer culture volume to 8 Greiner tubes of 50 ml and cool on ice for 15 minutes.
4.Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 2000g and 4°C.
5.Discard supernatant and add 5ml of 10% glycerol, resuspend the pellet in it and then add 45ml of 10% glycerol, shake by hand a few times to mix.
6.Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 2000g and 4°C.
7.Discard supernatant and add 5ml of 10% glycerol, resuspend the pellet in it and then add 45ml of 10% glycerol, shake by hand a few times to mix.
8.Put on ice for 10 minutes.
9.Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 2000g and 4°C.
10.Discard supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 ml ice-cold 10% glycerol, pool the contents of all 8 tubes into one tube.
11.Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 1500g and 4°C.
12.Discard supernatant and add 400 μl 10% glycerol (this will result in ~800 μl suspension).
13.Make aliquots of 40μl in pre-cooled eppendorf tubes and flash freeze with N2(l). Store at -80°C.
03:00:00 37 °C 0.5 Genome copies per ml, OD600 <between> 1.0 Genome copies per ml, OD600
